Video.summary: Captain Scott Walker fishes out of Hawks Cay with fellow Duck Key resident Captain Ariel Medero catching Blackfin Tunas and big Queen Snappers.
Video.title: "Tuna and Queens with Capt. Ariel"

Video.summary: New. York. City. That’s where our host Josh Rosen kicks off his journey in Episode 4—and where he lived for 16 years and started up the surf-centric apparel brand, Saturdays NYC. Back in his old stomping grounds, Josh first meets up with the award-winning chef and Mumbai native, Chintan Pandya, currently blowing up the New York food scene with his unapologetic and authentic Indian cuisine (pro tip: try the goat brains). Next, Josh travels through the boroughs, heads upstate, and along the coast to source local ingredients—from sake brewers and pig farmers to catching fresh lobster just 100 miles from Wall Street. As always the adventure will end with a feast, right back in Chintan’s kitchen.
Video.title: DIRT - "Exploring New York City's Hidden Culinary Gems: Cooking Goat Brains, Lobster & MORE"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Exploring New York City's Hidden Culinary Gems: Cooking Goat Brains, Lobster & MORE"
DIRT - "Exploring New York City's Hidden Culinary Gems: Cooking Goat Brains, Lobster & MORE"
DIRT - "Exploring New York City's Hidden Culinary Gems: Cooking Goat Brains, Lobster & MORE"

Video.summary: Captain Jonathan Moss looks back at some memorable entries from Season 6 of The Captain’s Log.
Video.title: "Memorable Entries"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger take Plantation Boat Mart’s new 36’ Invincible out to the swordfish grounds.
Video.title: "Swordfishing out of Plantation Boat Mart"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger break in their new Invincible Catamaran with a trip to fish out of Palm Beach with their friend Captain Dave Fields.
Video.title: "Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"

Image.alt: "Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"
"Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"
"Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fly kites with Captain Dave Fields for a great day of sailfishing out of Sailfish Marina in Palm Beach.
Video.title: "Palm Beach Sailfishing"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger take a long run out into the Gulf from Key West to sample the bottom fishing and the long range capability of their new Invincible Catamaran.
Video.title: "Way Out in the Gulf Part 1"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger continue fishing after taking a long run out into the Gulf from Key West to sample the bottom fishing and the long range capability of their new Invincible Catamaran.
Video.title: "Way Out in the Gulf Part 2"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fish out of Key West for tunas and sailfish while battling big seas and big sharks.
Video.title: "Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"

Image.alt: "Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"
"Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"
"Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"

Video.summary: The Odyssea Sportfishing family grows as Casey Villone comes aboard as Rush's new deck hand. While Rush gained a helping hand on his charters, he also scored a new "Fishing Mate" who is always game to bend a rod. With a day off and a report of Tuna offshore, the two head out to see if they can beat the sharks and land a Key West sushi dinner.
Video.title: "Fishing Mate"

Video.summary: Rush teams up with longtime friend and fellow angler Jesus Diaz for a well-earned "busman's holiday" in the beautiful waters of Key West. Even on a day off, these two can't resist seeing what's going to bite. Taking the wind and the tides into account, the duo maps out a strategic circuit through Key West's surrounding waters to target cobia, grouper, snapper, and tuna for a busy day "off" work.
Video.title: "Fishing With Zeus in Key West"

Video.summary: In this episode, Mark is joined by Matt Ogle, a proffesional bowler that Mark has known since Matt was 10 years old. Mark has been close to their family and has Matt grow into the bowling pro he is today. Matt was raised to love what he does…bassin and bowling the same way his Dad loved doing both. Matt's Dad actually ran the NABA Tournament Trail in KY for many years; Matt won the 2019 PBA Doubles Championship and the 2023 PBA Championship.
Video.title: "Bassin' & Bowling"

Video.summary: Join the Raskob family on a summertime adventure in the Florida Keys.
Video.title: "Raskob Lifestyle"

Video.summary: This episode is all about thanking our service men and women who have served or are still currently serving; Our pals Wes Thomas and Terry McWilliams join in to help thank these heroes, along with great companies like IN 811 and Centerpoint; special thanks to West Bogg Park for having us again this year.
Video.title: "Tribute to Heroes"

Video.summary: In Episode 3, Josh starts off in the Twin Cities where he meets up with Yia Vang, an award-winning Hmong chef and restaurant owner, bringing his family’s traditions and flavors to the American Midwest. As Josh heads up north to Duluth and the boundary waters, he’ll meet plenty of farmers and foragers—harvesting mushrooms, wild rice, and catching a few fish (and waves) along the way. It all wraps up with an epic feast hosted by Yia back in the cities.
Video.title: DIRT - "Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? Wild Foraging & Delicious Local Eats"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? Wild Foraging & Delicious Local Eats"
DIRT - "Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? Wild Foraging & Delicious Local Eats"
DIRT - "Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? Wild Foraging & Delicious Local Eats"

Video.summary: Brandon fishes an insane grouper bite with his good buddy from Sodium Fishing Gear.
Video.title: "Seabass"

Video.summary: Everything is bigger in Texas, including the stories. With the whitetail rut in the midwest winding down, the Winchester Life Crew of Chris Keefer, Casey Keefer, Nikki Boxler and Paul Sawyer head south to the Texas Panhandle with Winchesters in hand hoping to wrangle in a few Texas-sized giants.
Video.title: “Lonestar Legends”

Video.summary: The positive impact made by the shooting sports and recreational shooting to people across our country can’t be measured in just one story, so we have three. In Florida, Olympian Dania Vizzi trains at her home shooting facility and talk about how she went from ballerina to one of the best shotgun shooters in the world. In Nebraska, firearms instructor Thea Van Syoc has used the disciplines of competitive pistol shooting and training to help shape her life. Finally we’ll take a look into the National African American Gun Association and learn how one day at the range for Phillip Smith created the fastest growing firearm organization in the United States.
Video.title: “Shooting is Life”

Video.summary: Capt. Jonathan Moss is heading offshore into the Gulf of America, eager to feed his offshore fishing obsession.
Video.title: "Offshore Obsession"

Video.summary: Capt. Brandon Branch, Steve Grant and Kelly Player leave Jacksonville, FL for the Gulfstream to get into some Mahi.
Video.title: "Mahi Madness"

Video.summary: CA returns to his old stomping grounds, chasing legend in familiar waters. Giants still roam here and feed on the smallest of offerings.
Video.title: "The Hunt For Gobby Dick"

Video.summary: With the local Key West ship wrecks covered in Permit, Rush takes good friend and marine artist KC Scott fishing for "inspiration." While permit is the plan, the boys stay rigged and ready for the numerous species the Florida Keys may deliver when fishing offshore.
Video.title: "Permit Sketch"

Video.summary: The most important fish catch is the one that got you hooked on fishing in the first place, and for many of us that catch happened early in life. On this episode Ali and Rush grab their favorite little fishing buddies for a great day on the water on their respective coasts. In Florida, Rush spends time with his son Kade fishing for huge permit while Ali takes his nephew Duke out for some wholesome yellowtail action in California.
Video.title: "Hooked Early"

Video.summary: As the SoCal Bluefin Tuna fishing season begins to take shape, Ali and Rush head out to fish the waters of San Diego and see what new surprises may be in store for them this year. For many boats in the fleet, the heading turns to the south for a visit to the commercial tuna pen operation that often attracts other fish from miles away. It's parking lot fishing at its best, but with a change in technique and break from the crowd Ali puts Rush on the bite!
Video.title: "Bluefin Season GO"

Video.summary: When it comes to fishing and the marine industry, we are often more united by the Bluefin tuna than we think. For Ali Hussainy, the Bluefin is responsible for fueling his life long passion as an angler, and in this episode he gets to share that passion with two friends that have their own ties to the species. For wildlife biologist Forrest Galante and master sushi chef Hiroyuki Terada, catching a bluefin tuna on rod and reel will be a first!
Video.title: "United By Tuna"

Video.summary: Tanner and CA put their power fishing skills to the ultimate test.
Video.title: "Kitchen Sink"

Video.summary: Explore Pakistan's wilderness with Dan Cabela as he treks the rugged mountains, in search of ibex and urial, and learns about the community conservation model that has protected these species.
Video.title: "Community Conservation in Pakistan"

Video.summary: Discover the pressing threat of Chronic Wasting Disease in North American whitetail deer and the fierce debate around the strategies aimed at its management.
Video.title: "Battling Disease in Whitetail"

Video.summary: Nikki Boxler’s life in Update New York on her family farm raising cattle and producing maple syrup is a few thousand miles from the hills of New Mexico. We’ll take a look into Nikki’s life back home and then follow along as she visits the small town of Quemado and its bustling population of 228, while she pursues bugling elk with her favorite Winchester in hand. We’ll also head to Missouri and Texas to chase whitetails with Chris Keefer and Paul Sawyer.
Video.title: “New Mexico Magic”

Video.summary: We set out to tell the tale of Marion - and his legendary Mack’s Prairie Wings, smack dab in the middle of the world famous Stuttgart, Arkansas - the land of ducks and rice. Get an inside look at a man that many consider to be larger than life, from those that knew him best, like his cousin and fellow hell raiser, Bud McCollum. We’ll even hear a story or two from Milton Stovesand, an 81 year old former waterfowl guide and Mack’s employee who actually guided John Olin a time or two, and ran the timber holes with Marion. It’s timber holes and tall tales. It’s cups of coffee and cupped up wings. It’s partners in business, and partners in potholes and it’s pure Arkansas...just like Marion.
Video.title: “The Legend of Stuttgart”

Video.summary: The story of Team Winchester shooters Zach Kienbaum and Desirae Edmunds is told. Zach and Desi discuss how they got started in the shooting sports and how important coaching has become to both of them. They also talk shooting tips. Theo Ribbs breaks down the shotgun sports while Chris Keefer, Casey Keefer, Paul Sawyer, Nikki Boxler and Gabe Adair have a friendly sporting clays competition.
Video.title: “Clays and Competition”

Video.summary: The Pacific Northwest does not have the easiest conditions to train in for this shooting sports team. For this Seattle-based SST Ravens, this skeet and trap team finds that the challenging weather they train in comes in handy when competing. Also: Competition shooting tips form pro shooter Theo Ribbs.
Video.title: “Ink in the Sky”

Video.summary: Every spring brings a new opportunity to take a step forward & recall the memories of seasons past. World War II Veteran and retired Judge Arden Hill has spent most of his life chasing turkeys in the hills of Tennessee. Now in his 90’s, Arden doesn’t let age stand in the way of his love for spring turkey hunting. Also: patterning turkey shotguns and hunting spring gobblers.
Video.title: “The Veteran”

Video.summary: Winchester introduces the Shoot United brand aimed at offering useful information and tips to the millions of new shooters entering the shooting sports. New York native and Midland University competitive shotgun shooter Amy Cawley and her journey as a student-athlete is followed. Also: Theo Ribbs gives shooting tips for competition and Foster Bartholow reviews Winchester AA Diamond Grade.
Video.title: "Let’s Shoot United"

Video.summary: This week we join professional angler, Andrew Nordbye, for a cold day on Guntersville to catch some giant crappie; traveling through a major winter storm and arriving at an abandoned lake was the not-surprising scene for the day. Andrew's priorities are in order when it comes to his marriage, his faith, and his family, including just-arrived new babies...but he sill made time to squeeze in this day on water with us!
Video.title: "Alabama Artic Crappies"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared and Capt. Jake Leon hop in the bay boat in search of a - back country slam.
Video.title: "Bay Boat Slam"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared Raskob joins great friends Mitch Widom, Andy Yaffa, Chris Hamaway, and local Capt. Gonzalo Castillo for an epic day of Mahi fishing just offshore in Los Cabos, Mexico.
Video.title: "Cabo Mahi"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared Raskob joins Capt. Eric Reynolds & Jessica Ocasio on a sporty adventure offshore from Islamorada in search of Swordfish.
Video.title: "Swords"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve start the season with Capt. Scott Stoky chasing Islamorada Tunas and Mahis offshore out of Plantation Boat Mart.
Video.title: "Islamorada Blackfins And Mahis With Capt Scott Stoky"

Image.alt: "Islamorada Blackfins And Mahis With Capt Scott Stoky"
"Islamorada Blackfins And Mahis With Capt Scott Stoky"
"Islamorada Blackfins And Mahis With Capt Scott Stoky"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve catch a mixed bag bottom fishing off of Islamorada with Captain Scott Stoky.
Video.title: "Islamorada Mixed Bag"

Video.summary: Capt Brandon visits Quails Landing Plantation in South Georgia and his buddy Steven Redrick to do all things outdoors.
Video.title: "Quails Landing"

Video.summary: Brandon fishes with Robbie Crosnoe On Lake Henderson. These longtime buddies are dialed into the lake like no one else.
Video.title: "One-Two Punch"

Video.summary: On this episode, Capt. Danny joins his brother Nick for a day of fishing for redfish in some of their local waters, but in a tournament mindset. Tune in to see how the Allen’s go about strategizing a day of tournament fishing in hopes of getting some bragging rights and a check!
Video.title: "Tournament Trail"

Video.summary: Key West. The pulse of the Keys- it’s known for bars, nightlife and history above water. But the underwater world here can be just as lively. From the beautiful, resilient coral reefs to the crown jewel of the keys - the USS Vandenburg.
Video.title: "Key West"

Video.summary: In this episode, we will explore where the locals dive and experience the underwater gems where the natives love to hang out in the Florida Keys.
Video.title: "Dive like a Native"

Video.summary: We will take you to some of the wrecks in the Florida Keys that locals love to dive and share their rich history and the stories held deep aboard these sunken ships. We will also take a deep dive and discuss how these wrecks are critical to the environment.
Video.title: "Wrecks - Where Locals Love to Dive"

Video.summary: We will follow people rethinking how they can make a difference by volunteering to help the environment in the Florida Keys. Whether you are traveling to the Keys on vacation or you live there, people are making a difference by giving back.
Video.title: "Volunteering to help our Oceans"
